# Crosshair Component

The `<Crosshair>` component allows us to define a layer to render a crosshair at the mouse position. It is normally used in conjunction with the `<EventReceiver>` component to provide mouse events.

<Canvas>
    <Story id="components-crosshair--default" />
</Canvas>

### `<Crosshair>` Props

| Prop             | Type      | Default | Note                                         |
| ---------------- | --------- | ------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `showHorizontal` | `boolean` | `true`    | Should a horizontal crosshair line be shown? |
| `showVertical`   | `boolean` | `true`    | Should a vertical crosshair line be shown?   |

<br />

## Adding a Crosshair Programatically

To adjust the crosshair programatically you should call the `eventActions.mouseEnter` function or the `eventActions.mouseMove` function:

```
eventActions.mouseEnter({ offsetX: 50, offsetY: 100 });
eventActions.mouseMove({ offsetX: 52, offsetY: 102 });
```

## Visual Styling

The colour and styles of the Crosshair comes straight from the theme object.

It is possible to style the following properties by changing the `theme.crosshair` object:

| Property            | Type     | Default   | Note                                            |
| ------------------- | -------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `stroke`\*          | `string` | `"#000000"` | The color of the crosshair                      |
| `strokeDasharray`\* | `number` | `0`         | The dash array pattern to use for the crosshair |
| `strokeOpacity`     | `number` | `0.5`         | The opacity to use for the crosshair            |
| `strokeWidth`       | `number` | `1`         | The stroke width to use for the crosshair       |
